title: A Clockwork Origin

jokes.text: Cubert, you crapscallion!
jokes.setup: Professor addressing his son in what sounds like an angry tone
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'crap' and 'rapscallion' - silly pseudo-profanity
jokes.explanation: Typical Farnsworth wordplay - character-appropriate silly pseudo-swearing that's economical and mildly quotable

jokes.text: To the Science Mobile. You mean the ship? Yes. The Science Mobile. It's just that you've never called it that before, but okay.
jokes.setup: Professor dramatically declares they'll use 'The Science Mobile'
jokes.punchline: Someone points out he's never called it that, Professor insists anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven absurdity - Professor's tendency to make grandiose declarations that he immediately has to defend

jokes.text: I don't understand evolution, and I have to protect my kids from understanding it! We will not give in to the thinkers!
jokes.setup: Anti-evolution protestor explaining their position
jokes.punchline: Admits ignorance as qualification and frames thinking as the enemy
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical writing - the economy of 'we will not give in to the thinkers' perfectly captures anti-intellectual sentiment

jokes.text: You people are as loud as you are ignorant. Now get back on your turnip trucks and go home!
jokes.setup: Professor insulting the protestors
jokes.punchline: Twist on 'turnip truck' idiom - they literally arrived on turnip trucks
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that fits the Professor's pompous character, though the turnip truck reference is somewhat predictable

jokes.text: That is an insultingly accurate stereotype, sir!
jokes.setup: Protestor responding to the turnip truck insult
jokes.punchline: Admits the stereotype is accurate while being offended by it
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure - the self-aware admission of accuracy while maintaining offense is perfectly crafted

jokes.text: I cannot speak for you, sir, but my ancestors were not monkeys. They were orangutans. Hard-working, patriotic orangutans.
jokes.setup: Dr. Banjo correcting misconceptions about evolution
jokes.punchline: Specifies orangutans, then adds absurd patriotic qualifier
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from specific to absurd - the patriotic orangutans bit shows character's earnest ridiculousness

jokes.text: Hey, Professor, I'm a flying spaghetti monster. You seriously believe I'm descended from some kind of flightless manicotti?
jokes.setup: Bender questioning evolution
jokes.punchline: Flying Spaghetti Monster reference twisted with pasta evolution joke
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-reference combining Flying Spaghetti Monster meme with pasta-based evolution joke - good Bender characterization

jokes.text: I remind you that evolution is merely a theory, like gravity, or the shape of the Earth.
jokes.setup: Dr. Banjo dismissing evolution as 'just a theory'
jokes.punchline: Compares to gravity and Earth's shape - equally 'theoretical' established facts
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical jab at 'just a theory' argument - economical and perfectly structured escalation

jokes.text: Extended missing link argument sequence ending with 'Oh-ho! I've got you now.'
jokes.setup: Each fossil Farnsworth names, Banjo demands the link before it
jokes.punchline: Infinite regress argument structure with confident 'gotcha' moment
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating structure that builds absurdity while satirizing creationist debate tactics

jokes.text: Things don't exist simply because you believe in them. Thus sayeth the Almighty Creature in the Sky!
jokes.setup: Banjo making logical point about belief vs reality
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by citing his own belief-based deity
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure - character makes logical argument then immediately contradicts it with faith-based claim

jokes.text: I'll show that banana-swilling poop-slinger!
jokes.setup: Professor angrily responding to Dr. Banjo
jokes.punchline: Absurd monkey-based insult combining eating and throwing behaviors
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment - Professor's creative insults fit his personality but this one's not particularly clever

jokes.text: I found a missing link. It seems to be half man, half toucan. Not what we're looking for. Throw it in the soup!
jokes.setup: Amy excitedly reporting a discovery
jokes.punchline: Absurd half-toucan creature casually discarded into soup
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with dark casual disposal - economical and appropriately weird for Futurama

jokes.text: Uh-oh. It's another one of Fry's dogs. You find something, Hermes? Uh... no.
jokes.setup: Hermes finds something during dig
jokes.punchline: References Fry's emotional attachment to dogs, Hermes covers it up
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Hermes' protective instinct - works better for fans familiar with Fry's dog trauma

jokes.text: Oh, darn it! I broke off one of my of fingers! Look, Cubert. The neck on this one. I bet he spent a fortune on ties!
jokes.setup: Amy breaks off fossil finger, Professor examines long neck
jokes.punchline: Casual joke about extinct creature's hypothetical tie expenses
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about long-necked dinosaur - fits Professor's tendency to make inappropriate light comments

jokes.text: What, too soon? I highly doubt a Jurassic elaphrosaurid had access to neckwear.
jokes.setup: Professor makes tie joke, asks if too soon
jokes.punchline: Amy's deadpan scientific correction about dinosaur fashion access
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Professor's inappropriate humor met with Amy's scientific literalism

jokes.text: I knew I should have gone with the ring-around-the-collar joke.
jokes.setup: Professor reflecting on his failed tie joke
jokes.punchline: References old detergent commercial as alternative joke choice
jokes.explanation: Weak meta-joke about joke construction - dated reference doesn't add much humor

jokes.text: Hey, look! I found a robot fossil! That's a bedspring, you dumb bedspring!
jokes.setup: Bender excitedly claims to find robot fossil
jokes.punchline: It's just a bedspring, Professor insults him using that same object
jokes.explanation: Good economy in the insult structure - using the mistaken object as the insult itself is clever wordplay

jokes.text: I've hit a rich vein of missing links. Java Man, Piltdown Man, Manfred Mann.
jokes.setup: Professor listing important anthropological discoveries
jokes.punchline: Slips in 1960s rock band among the fossil hominids
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure - the 'Man' pattern builds to the musical punchline perfectly

jokes.text: What the...? Oh, that's mine.
jokes.setup: Professor discovers something shocking in his excavation
jokes.punchline: Deadpan admission that the 'fossil' is actually his own possession
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery - the casual admission after apparent excitement is well-structured

jokes.text: My tux doesn't fit. Probably because I've grown so much since I last wore it. Or evolved, one might say. One might not say that. Your tux doesn't fit because you stole it from a boy. You mean a man. It was his Bar Mitzvah.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to connect his tight tux to evolution
jokes.punchline: Reveals he stole it from a 13-year-old at his Bar Mitzvah
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-driven escalation - Fry's attempt at pseudo-science undercut by his criminal past

jokes.text: Welcome, museum members. Or as I like to call you, future exhibits.
jokes.setup: Museum director greeting members
jokes.punchline: Implies they'll all eventually be dead museum displays
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy - the morbid reframing is both funny and quotable

jokes.text: I don't want to live on this planet anymore.
jokes.setup: Professor seeing his discovery misrepresented as creationist evidence
jokes.punchline: His catchphrase of ultimate frustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment and highly quotable catchphrase - became a cultural meme

jokes.text: Professor, is this your only water source? It looks like Diet Dr. Pepper. It's not that bad. It's just laden with toxic minerals.
jokes.setup: Leela questioning the sketchy water quality
jokes.punchline: Professor's casual admission it's toxic after saying it's 'not that bad'
jokes.explanation: Standard setup-punchline structure with Professor's typical oblivious reassurance followed by alarming admission

jokes.text: Speaking of nasty irritants, what's gonna become of Cubert?
jokes.setup: Professor talking about nanobots eating irritants
jokes.punchline: Fry connects this to Cubert being an irritant
jokes.explanation: Good callback connection between nanobots and annoying child character

jokes.text: Cubert, I felt we needed some father-son bonding time, so I found a couple of baseball gloves and boiled them up for lunch.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg explaining his attempt at father-son bonding
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'baseball gloves' as food rather than sports equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg characterization - his misunderstanding of human customs leads to absurd literal interpretation

jokes.text: Wait! We still have to discuss the facts of life. What are they?!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying to have 'the talk' with Cubert
jokes.punchline: Reveals he doesn't know what the facts of life are either
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - Zoidberg's attempt at parental responsibility undermined by his own ignorance

jokes.text: Ah, the water's as sterile as my milkman-trusting father.
jokes.setup: Professor commenting on the purified water
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific comparison implying his father was cuckolded
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark and specific comparison - the randomness of the personal detail makes it funnier

jokes.text: Those robots evolved all by themselves, you say? It wasn't by themselves. I put them there. I'm a genius. Get over it.
jokes.setup: Professor arguing against evolution claim
jokes.punchline: Admits to creating them while telling others to 'get over' his genius
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor ego moment - simultaneously explaining and boasting while being dismissive

jokes.text: Oh, no! My sunglasses were in there!
jokes.setup: Robot creatures emerging from water looking threatening
jokes.punchline: Fry's concern is about his sunglasses, not the danger
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Fry's priorities - worries about accessories during crisis

jokes.text: Look, there's a cave-like hole in that mountain. It might be a cave.
jokes.setup: Character pointing out potential shelter
jokes.punchline: Redundant observation about cave-like hole possibly being a cave
jokes.explanation: Weak observational humor - the redundancy is mildly amusing but lacks cleverness

jokes.text: Pineapple?! Ugh! Oh, man! Oh! So much for that.
jokes.setup: Group discovers their only food is pineapple pizza
jokes.punchline: Universal disgust and immediate abandonment of the food
jokes.explanation: Standard pineapple pizza hate joke - relatable but not particularly clever or original

jokes.text: I remembered you liked superheroes, so I painted you a mural on your wall. This is Father-Man. He fights crime to earn Son-Boy's respect. Is it working? This is sucky! You suck! Who taught you to do three-point perspective? I could make a better mural with my butt!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg's earnest attempt to connect with Cubert through art
jokes.punchline: Cubert's brutal critique including technical art criticism
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Zoidberg's pathetic attempt met with Cubert's unexpectedly sophisticated art criticism

jokes.text: Father-Man away.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg's failed attempt to bond with Cubert
jokes.punchline: Dejected superhero-style exit line
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character consistency - Zoidberg maintains the superhero bit even in defeat

jokes.text: One thing about Bristol-Myers-Squibb... they know how to cook a steak. Hey, look at me. I'm the Tidy Bowl Man. I own a yacht, and everybody poops on me!
jokes.setup: Bender making random corporate endorsement
jokes.punchline: Tidy Bowl Man reference with yacht/toilet bowl confusion
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor mixing corporate references with scatological wordplay - somewhat random but fits Bender

jokes.text: Great Scott, a tricycle-tops.
jokes.setup: Professor seeing robotic dinosaur
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'tricycle' and 'triceratops'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay combining mechanical and prehistoric elements - economical and fits the robot evolution theme

jokes.text: I'm sorry for treating you like a total Zoidberg... I mean, loser.
jokes.setup: Cubert apologizing to Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Uses Zoidberg's name as synonymous with 'loser' then corrects himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that turns character's name into an insult then acknowledges it - perfect character integration

jokes.text: Why don't you ask your mom? She's coming over for a sex visit.
jokes.setup: Cubert being taunted by robot bully
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately explicit taunt about Cubert's mother
jokes.explanation: Standard crude bully humor - works for character type but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Don't worry, I know how to handle bullies. Just pretend like you're pathetic. Help, I'm scared! I wet myself. I'm crying like a baby and I'm soaking in pee, but what else is new?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg giving advice on handling bullies
jokes.punchline: Escalating self-deprecation ending with 'what else is new'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - his 'advice' is just accepting his pathetic state with resignation

jokes.text: Whoa! You're like some kind of Dumpster Jedi.
jokes.setup: Cubert impressed by Zoidberg's anti-bully technique
jokes.punchline: Combines 'dumpster diving' with 'Jedi' to describe Zoidberg's skills
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy combining pop culture reference with Zoidberg's trashy nature - highly quotable

jokes.text: If this is anything like killing that pigeon on my balcony, we've got our work cut out for us.
jokes.setup: Crew facing large robot dinosaur
jokes.punchline: Bender compares it to casually murdering a pigeon
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Bender's casual relationship with violence - fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: What the shmell happened? A mass extinction. That solar flare created a huge electromagnetic pulse that, while harmless to organic life, short-circuited the robo-dinosaurs. Convenient.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining the convenient robot dinosaur extinction
jokes.punchline: Bender's dry 'Convenient' comment on the plot device
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on plot convenience - Bender acknowledging the obviously contrived solution

jokes.text: Sweet robot swan of Botswana.
jokes.setup: Bender expressing surprise
jokes.punchline: Absurd robotic exclamation combining geography and waterfowl
jokes.explanation: Creative Bender exclamation - the randomness of 'Botswana' and specificity of 'robot swan' fits his character

jokes.text: I think they want wives, so just play along. If it doesn't work out, we'll still get half their rocks. I can earn my own rocks. Also, I don't want any rocks.
jokes.setup: Leela suggesting they pretend to be cave wives
jokes.punchline: Amy's modern feminist response about financial independence
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Amy's modern values applied to prehistoric situation with escalating rejection

jokes.text: I'm going to miss Spencer.
jokes.setup: Cave robot husbands have disappeared
jokes.punchline: Amy names one of the primitive robots 'Spencer'
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Amy's tendency to humanize and attach to anyone

jokes.text: Nice net. What, you... you can speak? Dread my locks.
jokes.setup: First contact with evolved robot civilization
jokes.punchline: 'Dread my locks' as robotic version of 'dreadlocks' greeting
jokes.explanation: Clever robotic take on Rastafarian culture with mechanical twist

jokes.text: I now present my latest discovery... the amazing non-mechanical man, Homo Farnsworth.
jokes.setup: Museum presentation of humans as specimens
jokes.punchline: Humans now classified as 'non-mechanical' curiosities
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal from earlier scene, humans now the exhibits

jokes.text: Thank you. I must say, I'm so proud to see what you've blossomed into since I first created your ancestors.
jokes.setup: Professor being honored by robot society
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals he created their entire civilization
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the revelation - Professor's casual mention of creating their civilization

jokes.text: I don't want to live on this planet anymore.
jokes.setup: Robot scientist facing evidence against evolution
jokes.punchline: Uses Professor's catchphrase in parallel situation
jokes.explanation: Effective callback showing the parallel situations - robot scientist having same reaction as human Professor

jokes.text: I don't want to live on this planet anymore.
jokes.setup: Robot scientist discovers creationist evidence
jokes.punchline: Uses Professor's signature phrase in robot evolution context
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reverses the situation - now robot says it about creationist evidence

jokes.text: Could you drag me by the restroom?
jokes.setup: Professor being arrested and dragged away
jokes.punchline: Polite request for bathroom break during arrest
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Professor's politeness even while being arrested for heresy

jokes.text: Bender, what the hell are you doing? Shut up, I'm billing you by the hour.
jokes.setup: Bender suddenly appearing as Professor's lawyer
jokes.punchline: Tells client to shut up because he's charging hourly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender greed motivation for representing client

jokes.text: Objection. In the absence of pants, defense's suspenders serve no purpose. I'm going to allow them... for now.
jokes.setup: Bender defending Professor without pants
jokes.punchline: Technical objection about suspender functionality
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist legal humor - the technical fashion objection in a serious trial setting

jokes.text: I ask you, is that so crazy? Yes, it's completely crazy, and that is why you must find him not guilty by reason of insanity!
jokes.setup: Bender asking if Professor's claims are crazy
jokes.punchline: Admits they're crazy then uses that for insanity defense
jokes.explanation: Good legal humor with perfect rhetorical reversal

jokes.text: Yes, it's completely crazy, and that is why you must find him not guilty by reason of insanity! Objection. I'm not crazy. I created you all, and I came here in a homemade spaceship and lived in a cave.
jokes.setup: Bender using insanity defense
jokes.punchline: Professor objects to being called crazy, then lists objectively crazy behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic structure - Professor defending his sanity by listing obviously insane actions

jokes.text: If you don't believe me, ask my uncle.
jokes.setup: Professor trying to prove his credibility
jokes.punchline: References Zoidberg as character witness despite their relationship
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but relatively weak - Zoidberg as character witness isn't particularly funny

jokes.text: Yeah, honey, I'll be home for dinner. The prosecution rests, Your Honor.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor supposed to give opening statement
jokes.punchline: Takes personal phone call instead, then immediately rests case
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist legal humor - the casual dismissal of professional duties for personal matters

jokes.text: Has the jury reached a verdict? No, we have not, for we have evolved to a higher state of consciousness. In the grand scheme, all physical beings are but yokels.
jokes.setup: Judge asking for jury verdict
jokes.punchline: Jury claims to have evolved beyond the need for legal proceedings
jokes.explanation: Creative evolution-themed resolution - jury literally evolving past the trial's relevance

jokes.text: That'll be $1P, 000.
jokes.setup: Bender billing for his legal services
jokes.punchline: Uses made-up currency notation for absurd amount
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender greed moment - the fake currency is mildly amusing but not exceptional

jokes.text: Good, 'cause I'm sick of him. I'm serious. He's a terrible person.
jokes.setup: Professor saying he's happy to raise his son
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's relief and brutal honesty about Cubert
jokes.explanation: Good dark ending - Zoidberg's honest assessment after attempting to bond with Cubert

tmdb_overview: The theory of evolution is put to the test on a planet inhabited by robots.
